mathematical_rationale: A crossing sequence at a tape boundary records the sequence of states the Turing machine enters whenever the head crosses that boundary. With a fixed finite state set Q, a crossing sequence of length c is a word of length c over Q. There are exactly |Q|^c such words. This cardinality bound quantifies the information bottleneck at any spatial cut, connecting continuous FRSB constraints to discrete communication capacity.
lean_4_code_delta:
import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Card
import Mathlib.Data.Vector.Basic

def CrossSequence (Q : Type) := List Q

def CrossSequenceOfLength (c : ℕ) (Q : Type) : Type := Fin c → Q

instance {c : ℕ} {Q : Type} [Fintype Q] : Fintype (CrossSequenceOfLength c Q) :=
  Pi.fintype (fun _ => Q)

theorem cross_sequence_card_bound {c : ℕ} {Q : Type} [Fintype Q] :
    Fintype.card (CrossSequenceOfLength c Q) = (Fintype.card Q) ^ c := by
  rw [Fintype.card_fun, Fintype.card_fin, Finset.prod_const, Finset.card_fin]
expected_verifier_state: Definitions `CrossSequence` (List-based) and `CrossSequenceOfLength` (length-indexed) added; theorem `cross_sequence_card_bound` proved, establishing that the number of length-c crossing sequences is exactly |Q|^c.
